text:
There once was a small town named Puddles that was famous for its extraordinary pool party every summer. People would come far and wide just to hang out by the gigantic beanbag chair that orbited the biggest inflatable duck.

It all started one balmy night when a girl named Heather brought her pet frog. The frog wanted to join the party, so Heather set little tiny ducklings around its feet.

As the night went on, the ducklings became a fully evolved family treefrog. People were amazed!

In fact, they even got a pair of them to eat at the dinner table the next evening. They turned out to be great chefs—especially at froggy soup.

The next summer, they invited frogs everywhere. There were froghouses everywhere. They had a park just for the frogs.

That's when the town decided to hold its annual Springtime Froglife Festival. Heather even got a grant to save the froggy forest.

And now, frogs everywhere play acoustic guitars on lazy summer
